title: [Calyx] Provide better way to identify the entry-point ComponentOp.
body:
Here we're checking to make sure an entry point function is defined. This may take any name, as shown by the parameter `topLevelFunction`. However, if I understand correctly, Calyx usually determines the entry point component by the name `main` (need confirmation on this @rachitnigam). 

In any case, that's how the Calyx dialect does it, e.g. 

https://github.com/llvm/circt/blob/f353f9126705a662d89ba9781f9d96f150633e90/lib/Dialect/Calyx/CalyxOps.cpp#L931-L932

If we don't want to use the name to determine whether it is the entry-point, we'll need to use something else, e.g. attribute or trait. This would also require verifying there is only a single entry point.

_Originally posted by @cgyurgyik in https://github.com/llvm/circt/pull/1813#discussion_r712309923_
